steps.explanation: Since \(K\) is the midpoint of \(\overline{JL}\), we have \(JK = KL = 13\), so \(JL = 26\) units.

\(\overline{LM}\) is a radius and \(\overline{JM}\) is the diameter of semicircle \(L\), meaning \(L\) is the center. So \(LM = r\) and \(JM = 2r\). Since \(JM = JL + LM\), we get \(2r = 26 + r\), giving \(r = 26\) units.

The figure consists of a rectangle and a semicircle. The rectangle's base runs from \(K\) to \(M\): \(KM = KL + LM = 13 + 26 = 39\) units. Its height is \(MN = 14\) units. Rectangle area \(= 39 \times 14 = 546\) square units.

Semicircle area with radius 26: \(\frac{1}{2}\pi(26)^2 = \frac{676\pi}{2} = 338\pi\) square units. Total figure area \(= 546 + 338\pi\).
  • \(546 + 676\pi\): Uses the full circle area instead of semicircle. Eliminated.
  • \(364 + 676\pi\): Wrong rectangle base and full circle. Eliminated.
  • \(546 + 338\pi\): Correct rectangle (39×14) and correct semicircle. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • \(364 + 338\pi\): Rectangle base of 26 instead of 39 (missing KL). Eliminated.
